ActionItems: Grow active membership before signing the renewed lease so the studio stays above break-even after the rent increase.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Break-even analysis prepared ahead of the studio lease renewal to evaluate how the landlord's proposed rent increase affects monthly profitability.

CurrentState.Note: Figures are based on the trailing three-month average of revenue and expenses.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: At the current membership rate, raising rent to $850 pushes the studio below break-even; membership must grow by roughly a dozen members to remain profitable.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Total monthly fixed costs divided by contribution margin per member gives the required active membership count at $750 rent.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Adds $150 to monthly fixed costs; break-even rises by a few members but the studio remains profitable.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increase to $750 per month

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Adds $250 to monthly fixed costs; the studio falls below break-even unless membership grows.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Rent increase to $850 per month
